script: Well, you know... when I was a child, I actually played outside every single day. We didn't have all these gadgets back then!

question.prompt: What is the speaker's real sentiment toward their childhood?
options: Nostalgic for a simpler time spent outdoors without modern technology.
Angry that they didn't have any gadgets to play with as a child.
Unsure if they actually played outside every day or not.
Actually asking the listener if they want to go outside now.

explanation: The contrast 'back then' vs. 'all these gadgets' highlights the speaker's nostalgic mood and pragmatic comparison of generations.
